title: LREM
description: Removes elements from a list. Deletes the list if the last element was removed.
complexity: O(N+M) where N is the length of the list and M is the number of elements removed.

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

content:
Removes the first `count` occurrences of elements equal to `element` from the list stored at `key`. The `count` argument influences the operation in the following ways: * `count > 0`: Remove elements equal to `element` moving from head to tail. * `count < 0`: Remove elements equal to `element` moving from tail to head. * `count = 0`: Remove all elements equal to `element`. For example, `LREM list -2 "hello"` will remove the last two occurrences of `"hello"` in the list stored at `list`. Note that non-existing keys are treated like empty lists, so when `key` does not exist, the command will always return `0`. ## Examples RPUSH mylist "hello" RPUSH mylist "hello" RPUSH mylist "foo" RPUSH mylist "hello" LREM mylist -2 "hello" LRANGE mylist 0 -1 ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information **RESP2:** [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of removed elements. **RESP3:** [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of removed elements.
intro:
Removes the first `count` occurrences of elements equal to `element` from the list
stored at `key`.
The `count` argument influences the operation in the following ways:

* `count > 0`: Remove elements equal to `element` moving from head to tail.
* `count < 0`: Remove elements equal to `element` moving from tail to head.
* `count = 0`: Remove all elements equal to `element`.

For example, `LREM list -2 "hello"` will remove the last two occurrences of
`"hello"` in the list stored at `list`.

Note that non-existing keys are treated like empty lists, so when `key` does not
exist, the command will always return `0`.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Removes the first `count` occurrences of elements equal to `element` from the list
stored at `key`.
The `count` argument influences the operation in the following ways:

* `count > 0`: Remove elements equal to `element` moving from head to tail.
* `count < 0`: Remove elements equal to `element` moving from tail to head.
* `count = 0`: Remove all elements equal to `element`.

For example, `LREM list -2 "hello"` will remove the last two occurrences of
`"hello"` in the list stored at `list`.

Note that non-existing keys are treated like empty lists, so when `key` does not
exist, the command will always return `0`.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
RPUSH mylist "hello"
RPUSH mylist "hello"
RPUSH mylist "foo"
RPUSH mylist "hello"
LREM mylist -2 "hello"
LRANGE mylist 0 -1

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
**RESP2:**

[Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of removed elements.

**RESP3:**

[Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of removed elements.
